name: imaging-data-commons
tagline: Queries and downloads public cancer imaging (DICOM CT/MR/PET, pathology) from NCI's Imaging Data Commons via the idc-index Python package.
verdict: No security red flags: no credential access, no obfuscated payloads, no exfiltration of local data, and it only pulls from mainstream PyPI/public buckets. Two hygiene issues for the user to know about: the file is 858 lines (~500-line budget), and the auto-upgrade check (line 26) does a buggy string comparison of version numbers and silently runs pip install

highlights.summary: Lines 18-33 run `subprocess.run([...pip3 install --upgrade --break-system-packages idc-index], check=True)` automatically as the mandated first step, silently modifying the Python environment without
No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g. when to prefer BigQuery/Portal instead, or when this skill does not apply) beyond a brief tool-selection table (lines 666-675).

Line 26 (`if installed < REQUIRED_VERSION`) does a lexicographic string comparison of version numbers, which can misclassify versions (e.g. '0.9.0' vs '0.11.14') and skip or trigger an upgrade incorre
